draft.introduction: John 16:7–33 continues the Farewell Discourse, focusing on the coming of the Paraclete (Spirit of truth) and the transformation of sorrow into joy. Jesus prepares his disciples for his departure and their coming persecution.

translation.text: Nevertheless, I tell you the truth: it is to your advantage that I go away, for if I do not go away, the Helper will not come to you. But if I go, I will send him to you. When the Spirit of truth comes, he will guide you into all the truth.
translation.original_text: ἀλλ’ ἐγὼ τὴν ἀλήθειαν λέγω ὑμῖν · συμφέρει ὑμῖν ἵνα ἐγὼ ἀπέλθω

translator_notes.note: The term Paraclete (παράκλητος) means “one called alongside”—variously translated as Advocate, Helper, or Comforter. In Islamic tradition, some scholars have identified the Paraclete with the Prophet Muhammad, reading “Periklytos” (praised one) for “Parakletos.”
